meaning: adj.催眠的,想睡的n.催眠剂,安眠药,使人昏昏沉沉的东西
examples: The election campaign was soporific but the results are startling.
Providence, out of pity for mankind, has instilled a soporific charm into all tedious things.
In contrast to the blaze of sunlight outside, the inner light had a soporific effect, especially on the twins.
But an election that had seemed a soporific canter has suddenly turned into a horse race.
Such feasts can be soporific, but unfortunately, so are the lectures, with undergraduate-level speakers sometimes reading from PowerPoint presentations.
